original_image_tags: The reverse side of a typed document, printed on aged, yellowish paper. Two dark punch holes are visible at the top edge. Faint, mirrored text from the obverse side bleeds through the paper. A single blue ink stamp is oriented upside down near the bottom.

elements.description: Two dark circular holes punched at the top. Faint, illegible text bleed-through from the reverse side. A blue stamp at the bottom reads 'RECEIVE', 'SEP 14 3 18 PM', 'F.B.I', and 'DEPT OF JUSTICE'. The page is blank apart from these elements.

judge.reason: Description B quotes specific, legible text from a stamp ('RECEIVE', 'SEP 14 3 18 PM', 'F.B.I', 'DEPT OF JUSTICE') while Description A states the stamp text is not readable, indicating a direct contradiction on the legibility and specific content of a key visual element.
